objective: Verify and finalize full runtime binding for target object DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, canonical_name: Директор) within the complete governance object chain GD -> DIR -> WRK. Prove Claude as the active runtime provider for DIR object runtime binding, establish auditor-validated executor dispatch to WRK-C1 as the downstream contour, and confirm idempotent Telegram routing for trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 through DIR managed channels DIR-C1.jsonl and DIR-C2.jsonl to WRK-C1.jsonl with trace-based deduplication.
assumptions: Target object DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, schema_version: 1.0, canonical_name: Директор, object_type: governance_object) is the mid-chain governance object with parent_object_id=GD and child_object_ids=[WRK] per objects_registry_v2.json (registry_id=objects_registry_v2, schema_version=1.0, protocol=BEM-931 v3.6). The full object chain is GD -> DIR -> WRK. DIR carries two registered contours: DIR-C1 (canonical_name: Контур правил директора, managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl) and DIR-C2 (canonical_name: Контур решений директора, managed_channel: governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl). Both contours contain roles ANALYST, AUDITOR, EXECUTOR. DIR object runtime binding must address both contours.

Prior probe trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1 completed with outcome=success (governance/state/provider_status.json v3: claude.last_trace=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1, claude.last_outcome=success, updated_at=2026-06-18T09:40:43Z). Artifacts governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json and governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json are on-disk (commit 686c12345a6d9f613e58371ddb2c7291541e955c). This dir_a2 probe is the binding-completion dispatch that acts on the dir_a1 plan, consuming executor and final auditor outputs to close the DIR binding cycle.

Claude is the active runtime provider for executor and auditor roles per governance/state/routing.json v6 (executor.active=claude, executor.primary=claude; auditor.active=claude, auditor.primary=claude). claude_code.status=ok per governance/state/provider_status.json v3. DIR passport currently declares runtime_provider=codex_local — a stale value predating the Claude binding initiative. DIR object runtime binding in this probe must be finalized under Claude provider; passport field is authoritative only after executor-side update under auditor approval.

WRK-C1 (contour_id=WRK-C1, managed_channel=governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl, canonical_name: Первый внутренний контур работника) is the canonical downstream executor contour for the full GD -> DIR -> WRK chain. WRK passport (governance/architecture/object_passports/WRK.json, parent_object_id=DIR, extension_policy.minimum_contours=3) confirms WRK-C1 as the primary contour. DIR runtime binding proof requires verified executor dispatch from DIR to WRK-C1.EXECUTOR via DIR routing path. WRK-C2 and WRK-C3 are auxiliary contours not targeted by this probe.

Idempotent Telegram routing for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 through DIR-C1.jsonl to WRK-C1.jsonl must produce exactly one executor entry on first delivery. A second replay with the same trace_id must be a no-op (line count of WRK-C1.jsonl must not increase). Deduplication key is trace_id scoped per managed_channel. This must be distinguished from the dir_a1 trace already routed in the prior probe — trace_ids are distinct and WRK-C1.jsonl must contain separate entries for each.

role_sequence.json task_type=object_runtime_binding maps to the internal_contour_proof sequence: analyst -> auditor -> executor -> auditor -> curator_summary. This dir_a2 probe occupies the curator_summary slot of cycle bem934_objects_bound_27750274693. The cycle_controls confirm max_role_steps_per_cycle=8; the prior dir_a1 and current dir_a2 dispatches are within the step budget. curator_id=DIR.CURATOR is the rule_owner for DIR object; runner governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py must be importable and syntactically valid.

GD object (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/GD.json, canonical_name: Генеральный директор, parent_object_id=null, child_object_ids=[DIR]) is the chain head. GD currently has status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING in the registry but its binding probe preceded DIR in the runner dispatch sequence. The GD probe result is the upstream pre-condition for DIR. WRK object (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/WRK.json, parent_object_id=DIR) remains PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING — WRK probe is dispatched only after DIR binding is committed and objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry shows status=BOUND.
steps: Step 1 — Artifact continuity pre-check for DIR (passport: governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json): Before any binding write, confirm dir_a1 artifacts are present and valid. Verify governance/proofs/BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json contains trace_id matching bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a1 and status=PASS with all semantic_checks true including mentions_dir_target_object, mentions_wrk_c1, mentions_claude_provider, mentions_object_runtime_binding, and mentions_idempotent_telegram_routing. Verify governance/proofs/BEM934_object_binding_plan.json references object DIR, both contours DIR-C1 and DIR-C2, downstream WRK-C1, and provider=claude. If either artifact is absent or has status≠PASS, raise blocker ARTIFACT_CONTINUITY_FAIL and halt dir_a2 sequence; escalate to curator per fallback_rules.on_role_blocked.

Step 2 — Claude provider runtime assertion for DIR object binding: Confirm governance/state/routing.json v6 executor.active=claude and auditor.active=claude. Confirm governance/state/provider_status.json v3 claude_code.status=ok and claude.last_outcome=success (last_trace=...dir_a1). Assert that DIR object runtime binding provider is claude, superseding the stale passport value codex_local. If routing.json shows a non-claude active executor, apply fallback rule on_provider_limit: switch_provider_keep_role before proceeding. Record provider_assertion=claude, provider_assertion_result=PASS in DIR binding evidence block for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2.

Step 3 — DIR-C1 and DIR-C2 channel readiness verification: Confirm governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl (DIR-C1 managed channel, Контур правил директора) and governance/channels/DIR-C2.jsonl (DIR-C2 managed channel, Контур решений директора) both exist and are writable. Append a channel_readiness_check probe entry bearing trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 to each channel. Verify both writes succeed. If a channel file is absent, create it before writing and record channel_created=True in the evidence block. Record channel_check_DIR_C1=PASS and channel_check_DIR_C2=PASS. Absence of a channel is not a blocker but triggers cold-start binding annotation for that DIR contour.

Step 4 — Auditor pre-check for DIR runtime binding with WRK-C1 downstream route verification: Run auditor_stage_runner in isolated tempdir targeting DIR. Confirm executor dispatch resolves to WRK-C1.EXECUTOR via DIR routing path (to=WRK-C1.EXECUTOR, routed_through=DIR, dir_source=True). Verify wrk_c1_to_executor.jsonl is written with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 in the tempdir. Confirm all auditor pre-checks: real_pre_channel_consumed=True, executor_route_selected=True, semantic_pre_check_pass=True, trace_preserved=True, result_artifact_emitted=True. Record auditor_pre_dir_a2=PASS. Failure of any check halts binding execution and returns to executor per fallback_rules.on_audit_rejected.

Step 5 — DIR binding evidence record creation at governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding.json: Write the DIR binding evidence record containing: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, provider=claude, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, runner_check=PASS (governance/runners/dir_curator_runner.py importable, no SyntaxError/ImportError, DIR.CURATOR declared as rule_owner), source_receipt_blob_sha (from dir_a1 materialization receipt), source_plan_blob_sha (from BEM934_object_binding_plan.json), source_commit_sha=686c12345a6d9f613e58371ddb2c7291541e955c, status=BOUND. This record is the canonical proof artifact for DIR object runtime binding.

Step 6 — Idempotent Telegram routing verification for DIR trace bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2: Route trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 through the Telegram routing layer targeting governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl. Record baseline line count L0. Deliver once: confirm exactly one new entry is written (count = L0+1). Deliver a second time with the same trace_id: confirm count remains L0+1 (no additional entry written). Verify that an existing dir_a1 entry in WRK-C1.jsonl is unaffected — trace_ids differ and no cross-trace collision occurs. Record telegram_idempotency_dir_a2=PASS.

Step 7 — Chain continuity confirmation and objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry update: Verify full chain state: GD passport parent_object_id=null (chain head, binding confirmed prior to this probe), DIR passport object_id=DIR, child_object_ids=[WRK] (mid-chain, bound in this step), WRK passport parent_object_id=DIR, status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING (WRK probe is next). Update objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry with runtime_binding block: status=BOUND, provider=claude, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, bound_at=<utc_now>, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json. GD entry retains its BOUND block from prior probe. WRK entry remains PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING until WRK probe completes. Sequential dispatch is enforced — WRK probe must not be queued until DIR registry write is committed.

Step 8 — Curator summary closure and results transport: Append final DIR completed record to governance/transport/results.jsonl with fields: trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, role=curator, provider=claude, object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, chain=GD->DIR->WRK, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, status=completed, blocker=null, commit_sha=<executor step commit>. Write curator report to governance/reports/bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2.md with clean operator-facing status (no diff, no stdout, no file paths beyond report path). DIR object runtime binding cycle is closed. Initiator receives status, next step (WRK probe), and no blocker.
acceptance: governance/proofs/bem934_objects_bound/DIR_runtime_binding.json is present and contains: object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, provider=claude, workflow=.github/workflows/claude.yml, contours_bound=[DIR-C1, DIR-C2], downstream_contour=WRK-C1, status=BOUND, runner_check=PASS, source_commit_sha non-empty, source_receipt_blob_sha non-empty.

objects_registry_v2.json DIR entry carries runtime_binding block with status=BOUND, provider=claude, trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, and explicitly references passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json. GD entry is BOUND from prior probe. WRK entry remains PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING awaiting WRK probe. Registry contains no concurrent write for WRK in this step.

governance/channels/WRK-C1.jsonl contains exactly one entry for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2 after two Telegram routing replay deliveries; line count does not increase on second delivery. A separate entry for trace dir_a1 is present and unaffected. Idempotency for DIR trace is confirmed in the WRK-C1 downstream channel.

DIR-C1.jsonl and DIR-C2.jsonl both contain channel_readiness_check entries for trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, confirming both DIR contours are active and writable under Claude provider binding. Channel files exist on disk after Step 3 (created if previously absent).

governance/transport/results.jsonl contains a completed record with trace_id=bem934_objects_bound_27750274693_dir_a2, role=curator, provider=claude, object_id=DIR, passport_path=governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json, status=completed, blocker=null, and non-empty commit_sha referencing the DIR binding executor commit.
risks: DIR passport stale runtime_provider field (codex_local): The passport at governance/architecture/object_passports/DIR.json declares runtime_provider=codex_local. Materializer or auditor checks reading the passport directly may reject Claude as an unauthorized provider for DIR. Mitigation: Step 2 asserts provider exclusively from routing.json and provider_status.json, not from the DIR passport field. The binding evidence record DIR_runtime_binding.json is the authoritative provider claim; passport field update is an executor-side write deferred to Step 7 under auditor approval.

dir_a1 artifact dependency: dir_a2 binding completion depends on dir_a1 artifacts (BEM934_object_binding_plan.json, BEM934_claude_result_materialization_receipt.json) being present, valid, and bearing status=PASS. If the dir_a1 executor commit was reverted or artifacts are absent, Step 1 raises blocker ARTIFACT_CONTINUITY_FAIL. Mitigation: Step 1 performs explicit artifact pre-check before any binding write; blocker escalates to curator per fallback_rules.on_role_blocked.

WRK-C1 idempotency cross-trace contamination: WRK-C1.jsonl may already contain an entry from dir_a1 routing. Risk that deduplication logic incorrectly treats dir_a1 and dir_a2 as the same trace and suppresses the dir_a2 write. Mitigation: Step 6 explicitly verifies a new distinct entry IS written on first dir_a2 delivery (trace_ids ...dir_a1 ≠ ...dir_a2 are different strings), confirming per-trace scoping in the deduplication layer.

Concurrent WRK probe race condition: bem934_objects_bound_runner may dispatch the WRK probe before the DIR binding registry write commits. Risk: objects_registry_v2.json write conflict or WRK probe reads stale DIR status=PENDING_RUNTIME_BINDING. Mitigation: Step 7 enforces sequential dispatch — WRK probe must not be queued until DIR entry shows status=BOUND in objects_registry_v2.json and the registry commit SHA is recorded in DIR_runtime_binding.json.

DIR-C1 and DIR-C2 channel file absence: governance/channels/DIR-C1.jsonl and DIR-C2.jsonl may not exist on disk. Risk: Step 3 channel-write verification fails with FileNotFoundError. Mitigation: Step 3 creates channel files if absent before writing and records channel_created=True in the evidence block. Absence is treated as cold-start binding for DIR contours, not a fatal error.
